title: Prove every configured Codex repo hook through the installed command route
action: Add a process-level installed-route harness without changing runtime/config behavior. Parse all nine command handlers from the actual .codex/hooks.json and execute those command strings exactly through `${SHELL:-/bin/bash} -lc`, matching Codex's documented session-cwd/login-shell contract rather than invoking wrappers directly. Run only inside an isolated temporary git fixture whose root path contains spaces and Unicode, with a nested working directory, copied current hooks plus required bee helper/config state, and CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT unset. Add a `--repo-route-only` mode plus a bounded config-ref sensitivity option. Before accepting GREEN, run the new rows against pre-fix ref `ba31819cea42e3f4dcfd294f05778caac21d8b5d` and record the expected RED old-path/Stop failure in the cell trace; the same rows must then pass against the active config. Cover every configured event/handler, not only Stop. Force both Stop handlers to produce non-empty output and require exit 0 plus parseable JSON containing systemMessage and no decision:block. Execute the configured PreToolUse transport itself with an apply_patch targeting .bee/state.json under an unapproved gate and require exit 2. From a non-git cwd require the configured transport to emit its visible setup diagnostic and exit 0. Hash the live repository's .bee/state.json and the presence/bytes of .bee/.inject-cache.json and .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl before/after; fixture execution must leave them identical. Rerun a parseable plugin census and block if a bee plugin appeared. Do not trust hooks or touch any runtime file; human /hooks review and fresh-event UAT remain Gate 4.

truths: all nine commands loaded from the active .codex/hooks.json execute through the same login-shell and session-cwd route Codex uses
root and nested working directories inside a spaces/Unicode git fixture succeed with both Claude root variables absent
both configured Stop handlers exit 0 and every non-empty stdout parses as JSON systemMessage with no decision:block
the configured PreToolUse command, not a direct wrapper invocation, denies the gated apply_patch with exit 2

a non-git working directory exercises visible transport fail-open: exit 0, stdout EMPTY, and stderr contains the exact literal `bee: hook transport unavailable (no git root)` pinned by codex-parity-6a

GIT-ABSENT ROW (blocker F2(b), previously prohibited in error): the configured command is spawned with `git` shimmed OFF the PATH and must exit 0 with the same stderr literal and empty stdout. This is the branch that collapses `$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)` to empty and reproduces the EXACT MODULE_NOT_FOUND this slice exists to repair — it is the single most important row in the cell and must not be skipped

the route harness records RED against pre-fix config ref ba31819cea42e3f4dcfd294f05778caac21d8b5d and GREEN against the active config — NOTE (verified 2026-07-12): .codex/hooks.json in the working tree is BYTE-IDENTICAL to that ref until codex-parity-6a regenerates it, so this RED/GREEN split only materializes on a tree where 6a has LANDED. If `git show ba31819cea42e3f4dcfd294f05778caac21d8b5d:.codex/hooks.json` still matches the working-tree file, STOP and return [BLOCKED] — never report a RED you did not observe
fixture execution leaves live bee state, injection cache, and hook log presence/bytes unchanged
plugin census remains parseable and shows no bee plugin

--repo-route-only is a REAL mode, not a silently-ignored flag: with the strict-flag parsing landed by codex-parity-6a, running the suite with an unknown flag exits non-zero, so this cell's verify cannot pass green while the harness is unimplemented (verified 2026-07-12: today `node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --repo-route-only` exits 0 with ALL PASS because unknown flags are ignored)

artifacts.substantive: isolated process harness that parses and exercises every active repo command with RED sensitivity and live-state immutability guards

key_links: the harness reads .codex/hooks.json rather than reconstructing command strings
each configured command executes through the selected login shell with fixture cwd and fixture stdin payload
the pre-fix git ref feeds the same assertions used for the active config so sensitivity is mechanical
prohibitions: do not edit .codex/hooks.json, hooks/catalog.mjs, any wrapper/helper, plugin/global config, trust state, onboarding, or manifests
do not execute regression payloads against the live repository's workflow state or accept a direct-wrapper-only test as installed-route proof
do not weaken, delete, skip silently, or string-grep around existing hook contract rows

do not claim native Windows, non-POSIX shells (fish/nu $SHELL), or live post-trust firing as proved — but a PATH-shimmed git-absent row IS REQUIRED and must be proved (see truths); pin the route spawn to `bash -lc` rather than the dev's real $SHELL, and point HOME/CODEX_HOME at the fixture so an rc-file `cd` cannot let git-root resolution escape into the live repo

do not register any required row as skip:true — skip rows carry pass:true (test_hook_contracts.mjs:626-628) and are excluded from the failures filter (:1022), so a skipped row prints ALL PASS and exits 0; the route rows and the git-absent row must be REQUIRED (absent or skipped => non-zero)
do not touch concurrent .bee/config.json, dispatch-log, spike, E3/E4, or fanout-delegation work

verify: node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --repo-route-only && ! node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs

trace.outcome: Added --repo-route-only: a real process-level harness that parses all 9 commands from the ACTIVE .codex/hooks.json and runs each VERBATIM through /bin/bash -lc (the Codex transport), inside a throwaway git fixture whose root carries spaces+Unicode, from root and nested cwds, with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT unset and HOME/CODEX_HOME caged. Every configured event/handler is covered on 4 arms (root, nested, non-git cwd, and git SHIMMED OFF THE PATH — the branch that reproduces the original MODULE_NOT_FOUND), plus both Stop handlers, the configured PreToolUse apply_patch deny (exit 2), live-state immutability, and a plugin census. The required-row manifest is DERIVED FROM THE CONFIG, so a dropped or skipped route row exits non-zero (both proved by sabotage builds). RED observed against pre-fix ref ba31819 (39/47 fail, MODULE_NOT_FOUND, both Stop handlers dead), GREEN against the active config.

deviations.rule: auto-fix (deviation rule 1) — cell action says "Force BOTH Stop handlers to produce non-empty output"
deviations.what: bee-state-sync has NO stdout path at all: it is silent by contract (hooks/bee-state-sync.mjs header, cell codex-parity-3 — 'Always silent — it never emits stdout, so the Codex SubagentStop/Stop JSON-output requirement is satisfied by silence'). Forcing it to print would require editing a wrapper, which this cell prohibits (behavior_change: false).
deviations.resolution: Honored the binding must_haves TRUTH ('both configured Stop handlers exit 0 and every non-empty stdout parses as JSON systemMessage with no decision:block') rather than the action's stronger prose. Row route-stop-handler:session-close requires exit 0 + NON-EMPTY stdout + JSON systemMessage + no decision:block (the fixture — mid-phase, no HANDOFF — forces the 'hive door open' advisory). Row route-stop-handler:state-sync requires exit 0 + empty stdout AND that the run actually mutated the FIXTURE .bee/state.json (last_activity), so its silence is proved to be real work rather than a dead command. Both rows are REQUIRED.

deviations.rule: auto-fix (deviation rule 3) — payload defect found while building the PreToolUse row
deviations.what: The pre-existing APPLY_PATCH_PAYLOAD writes the target as the literal '.bee/state.json'. Codex apply_patch addresses files RELATIVE TO THE SESSION CWD, so from the nested fixture cwd that string denotes <nested>/.bee/state.json — not the bee state file — and the write-guard correctly ALLOWED it (exit 0). A naive reading would have logged this as a guard bug.
deviations.resolution: Added routeApplyPatchPayload(cwd, fixtureRoot), which writes the target relative to the row's cwd ('.bee/state.json' from the root, '../../.bee/state.json' from the nested cwd). The configured PreToolUse command now denies with exit 2 from BOTH cwds, which additionally proves the guard RESOLVES the target rather than string-matching it. The original APPLY_PATCH_PAYLOAD and every row that uses it are untouched.

deviations.rule: auto-add (deviation rule 2) — the required-row manifest as inherited was still evadable
deviations.what: 6a's manifest is a static id list. Had the route rows registered their own required ids as a side effect of being built, deleting an entire arm (e.g. the git-absent one) would have deleted its own requirement with it and the suite would have gone green on less work — the same class of hole that sank the predecessor cell.
deviations.resolution: routeRequiredRowIds() derives the manifest FROM .codex/hooks.json (4 arm rows per configured command + 1 handler row per Stop command + 7 fixed rows), independently of the rows actually produced. Proved by two sabotage builds: dropping the 9 git-absent rows => 9x REQUIRED ROW ABSENT, exit 1; marking them skip:true => 9x REQUIRED ROW SKIPPED, exit 1.

trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs — 73-row default suite (7-wrapper adversarial table + catalog-drift + codex-acceptance + adapter rows); every row spawns a wrapper DIRECTLY (node hooks/bee-*.mjs), which is precisely why it stayed green through the incident: the wrappers were fine, the INSTALLED COMMAND STRING was broken",
    "hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:626-628/:1022 escape hatch (skip:true rows are built with pass:true and are excluded from the failures filter) — the defect the predecessor cell was rejected for",
    ".codex/hooks.json (9 commands landed by codex-parity-6a), hooks/adapter.mjs (root discovery, advisory encoding), hooks/bee-state-sync.mjs (silent by contract), hooks/bee-session-close.mjs (Stop 'hive door open' advisory)"
  ],
  "tests_added": [
    "--repo-route-only: a REAL mode (46 rows, 0 skipped) that parses all 9 commands out of the ACTIVE .codex/hooks.json and executes each command string VERBATIM through `/bin/bash -lc` — the documented Codex transport (validation A1: the shipped binary spawns $SHELL -lc) — never a direct wrapper call",
    "4 arms per configured command: cwd=fixture root; cwd=nested 'src/deep nest ✦'; cwd=non-git dir; and git SHIMMED OFF THE PATH (a shim dir carrying node+bash+sh+coreutils but NOT git, with route-gitabsent-shim-precondition asserting `command -v git` is EMPTY under it before any conclusion is drawn — the trap that produced two false failures for the orchestrator)",
    "route-stop-handler:session-close (exit 0 + NON-EMPTY stdout + JSON systemMessage + no decision:block) and route-stop-handler:state-sync (exit 0 + silent + FIXTURE state.json actually mutated)",
    "route-pretooluse-applypatch-deny: the CONFIGURED PreToolUse command string, through the login shell, denies an apply_patch targeting .bee/state.json with exit 2 + reason",
    "route-live-state-untouched: sha256 of the live .bee/state.json + presence/bytes of .bee/.inject-cache.json and .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl, compared before/after; route-plugin-census: parseable `codex plugin list --json` with no bee plugin in the real Codex home",
    "--config-ref=<git-ref>: a bounded RED-sensitivity probe (rejected with exit 2 unless --repo-route-only), carrying route-config-ref-differs-from-worktree, which FAILS if the ref is byte-identical to the worktree so a RED can never be fabricated",
    "routeRequiredRowIds(): a required-row manifest DERIVED FROM THE CONFIG (not from the rows produced) — every configured command owes 4 arm rows, every Stop command owes a handler row; absent or skipped => non-zero"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": {
    "how_obtained": "node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --repo-route-only --config-ref=ba31819cea42e3f4dcfd294f05778caac21d8b5d — the SAME rows, fed the pre-6a .codex/hooks.json. Precondition row route-config-ref-differs-from-worktree passed first: that ref carries a DIFFERENT config than the worktree (post-6a, commit f0860ac), so the RED is real and observed, not asserted.",
    "result": "exit 1 — 39 of 47 rows FAIL",
    "verbatim": [
      "FAIL route-stop-handler:session-close :: expected exit 0 + non-empty stdout parsing as a JSON systemMessage with no decision:\"block\"; got status=1 stdout= stderr=node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1459",
      "FAIL route-stop-handler:state-sync :: expected exit 0 + empty stdout + a mutated fixture .bee/state.json; got status=1 stdout= stateMutated=false stderr=node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1459",
      "FAIL route-gitabsent:Stop:session-close :: [git shimmed OFF the PATH — the exact MODULE_NOT_FOUND branch] expected exit 0 (fail-open), got status=1 | stderr does not carry the pinned literal \"bee: hook transport unavailable (no git root)\"",
      "FAIL route-root:PreToolUse:write-guard :: expected exit 2 with a deny reason ...; got status=1 stderr=node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1459"
    ],
    "diagnosis": "The pre-6a commands are `node \"$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"/.bee/bin/hooks/bee-*.mjs`. Codex sets no CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, so the path collapses to /.bee/bin/hooks/... => MODULE_NOT_FOUND, exit 1: every hook dead, both Stop handlers dead, the write guard not guarding. That is the exact incident this slice exists to repair, reproduced mechanically by the new rows and cleared by the 6a transport.",
    "anti_fake_controls": [
      "sabotage build A — the 9 route-gitabsent rows deleted from the results: 9x 'REQUIRED ROW ABSENT', exit 1",
      "sabotage build B — the 9 route-gitabsent rows marked skip:true (the skip=>pass:true escape hatch): 9x 'REQUIRED ROW SKIPPED', exit 1",
      "`! node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag` still exits 2, so --repo-route-only is proved to be a real mode rather than a silently ignored flag"
    ]
  },
  "verification_run": {
    "command": "node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --repo-route-only && ! node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs",
    "result": "exit 0 — route mode 46 rows / 0 skipped / 0 failing; unknown flag exits 2; bare default suite 119 rows / 0 skipped / 0 failing (the pre-existing 73 rows untouched and still green, plus the 46 route rows); test_lib 169 passed 0 failed; test_onboard_bee PASS (0 failures, 1 skipped)",
    "isolation": "route-live-state-untouched passed in BOTH the green and the red runs: the live .bee/state.json hash and the presence/bytes of .bee/.inject-cache.json and .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl were identical before and after, even though these rows drive real state-mutating hooks."
  },
  "declared_gaps": [
    "The route spawn is pinned to /bin/bash -lc, not the developer's real $SHELL: a fish/nu $SHELL cannot run this POSIX transport. Declared, not proved (validation constraint 3).",
    "Native Windows is out of scope (validation constraint 3 / F3).",
    "Live post-trust firing inside a real Codex thread remains the Gate-4 human step: rewriting .codex/hooks.json invalidates the 9 trusted_hash entries, so a human must re-trust via /hooks (validation A3 / constraint 5)."
  ]
}
